column8: Jane was upset after she finished the regional swimming competition. She had practised every day for several month, but despite all her hard work, she finished in___________place. (TWO)
column9: She tried not to be too disappointed about it, but at her swimming practice the other day she___________her fastest time ever, which had made her really hopeful of winning the competition this time. (SWIM)
column10: Her father tried to make her fell better. "There are plenty of other swimming competitions to win. Just keep practising and one day you____________the gold!" he told her, making her laugh by stretching his arms up in the air so vigorously that he fell off his chair. (GET)
column11: We might not think about it much, but most of us owe a lot of the shipping industry. Almost anyone who__________a car, clothes or furniture recently has benefitted from the industry, because it is the cheapest way to transport goods. (BUY)
column12: No matter where a ship has sailed during its life, most ships_____________to South Asia when they are no longer usable. India, Bangladesh and Pakistan alone take up to 70% of worn-out ships. (SEND)
column13: The ships rest along the shoreline, where workers climb on them and take them apart piece by piece. In 2012, observers recorded that 365 ships_____________on beaches in this region. (LIE)
column14: The work is quite dangerous and occasionally, workers lose______________lives. More must be done to make this work safe. (THEY)
